title: [phase:critique] [family:reviewer-lane] [provider_hint:codex-sidecar] Review the auth-hardening remaining-task changes only. Files: /Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts, /Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts, /Users/masayuki/...
summary_text: 1. High: the happy-web refresh flow requires a JS-readable long-lived refresh token, which weakens the auth-hardening goal instead of strengthening it. The client reads and updates `config.refreshToken` directly in [happy-endpoint-client.js:55](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L55) and [happy-endpoint-client.js:75](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L75). In the companion runtime wiring, that token is sourced from `window.__HAPPY_RUNTIME_CONFIG__` or a `<meta>` tag at [happy-runtime-config.js:35](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/config/happy-runtime-config.js#L35). That makes the refresh credential available to any XSS-capable script, which is a materially bigger secret than the short-lived access token. 2. Medium: the worker refresh path can destroy a valid session on transient backend failure and report the wrong error class. `rotateRefreshToken()` consumes the old token before the later auth-state/read path completes at [jwt-auth.ts:323](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts#L323) and [jwt-auth.ts:338](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/utils/jwt-auth.ts#L338). Then `handleRefreshToken()` does another lookup via `getUserAuthState()` and converts `null` into `404 User not found` at [cockpit-api.ts:530](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L530) and [cockpit-api.ts:533](/Users/masayuki/Dev/cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy/src/handlers/cockpit-api.ts#L533). A D1 lookup failure after consumption becomes an apparent user-missing condition, and the client cannot retry with the old refresh token because it is already gone. 3. Medium: refresh requests still send the current bearer token, which is unnecessary and can break stricter refresh endpoints. `runRequest()` always injects `Authorization` from `config.authToken` at [happy-endpoint-client.js:42](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L42), and `refreshAuthToken()` uses that same helper for the refresh call at [happy-endpoint-client.js:63](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L63). If the refresh endpoint is intentionally unauthenticated or rejects expired bearer tokens early, this client-side refresh will fail for the wrong reason. Targeted verification I ran: `npx vitest run src/utils/jwt-auth.test.ts src/handlers/cockpit-api.auth.test.ts` in `cloudflare-workers-hub-deploy` and `node tests/test-endpoint-client.js` in `apps/happy-web`; both passed. Lane evidence: local diff/test review plus parallel sidecar review of the Cloudflare auth files and parallel sidecar review of the happy-web client/tests.
